stories.title: Migration: add description column + normalize categories
stories.description: Create SQL migration that: (1) adds `description TEXT` column to `metric_translations` table, (2) normalizes all metric_definitions categories from mixed English/Turkish to consistent Turkish slugs. Run the migration against the Neon TEST branch (URL in prd.json neon_test_branch), and save the SQL file to `db-schema/data/metric-translations/MT-001-migration.sql`.
acceptanceCriteria: `description` column exists on `metric_translations` (nullable TEXT)
All categories normalized per categoryMapping above
SQL file saved to `db-schema/data/metric-translations/MT-001-migration.sql`
Migration applied successfully to Neon test branch
Verify: SELECT DISTINCT category FROM metric_definitions ORDER BY category — no English category names remain

stories.title: Turkish descriptions: hemogram (36 metrics)
stories.description: Generate Turkish patient-facing descriptions for all metrics WHERE category='hemogram' (after normalization). Query the test DB, then for each metric generate a description following the DESCRIPTION_PROMPT in prompt.md. UPDATE metric_translations SET description=... WHERE locale='tr'. Save all SQL to file.
acceptanceCriteria: All hemogram metrics have Turkish descriptions
Each description: max 2 sentences, max 30 words, no disease names, function-first
SQL file saved and applied to test branch
Verify count matches

stories.title: Turkish descriptions: biyokimya (24 metrics)
stories.description: Generate Turkish descriptions for all metrics WHERE category='biyokimya'. Follow DESCRIPTION_PROMPT. Save SQL.
acceptanceCriteria: All biyokimya metrics have Turkish descriptions
Descriptions follow approved rules
SQL file saved and applied to test branch

stories.title: Turkish descriptions: idrar (28 metrics)

stories.title: Turkish descriptions: immunoloji (12) + kan_gazi (12)
stories.description: Generate Turkish descriptions for all metrics WHERE category IN ('immunoloji','kan_gazi'). Follow DESCRIPTION_PROMPT. Save SQL.
acceptanceCriteria: All 24 metrics have Turkish descriptions
Descriptions follow approved rules
SQL file saved and applied to test branch

stories.title: Turkish descriptions: remaining categories (43 metrics)
stories.description: Generate Turkish descriptions for ALL remaining categories: lipid, karaciger, koagulasyon, demir, tiroid, vitamin, hormon, diger, enflamasyon, tumor_belirtecleri, kardiyak. Follow DESCRIPTION_PROMPT. Save SQL. After this story, ALL 155 metrics should have Turkish descriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: All remaining metrics have Turkish descriptions
Descriptions follow approved rules
SQL file saved and applied to test branch
VERIFY TOTAL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM metric_translations WHERE locale='tr' AND description IS NOT NULL — must be 155

stories.title: English (EN) translations: display_name + description for all 155 metrics
stories.description: Add English translations for all 155 metrics. Query Turkish display_name and description from test DB as reference. Generate proper English medical display_name and description. INSERT with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. Save SQL.
acceptanceCriteria: 155 English rows with both display_name and description
Display names use standard English medical terminology
Descriptions follow same ethical rules as Turkish, in natural English
SQL file saved and applied to test branch

stories.title: Verification + create PR
stories.description: Run comprehensive verification against test DB: (1) all 6 locales have 155 translations each, (2) all translations have display_name AND description, (3) categories are normalized, (4) no description contains disease names. Then npm run build. Create PR.
acceptanceCriteria: SELECT locale, COUNT(*) FROM metric_translations GROUP BY locale — all 6 locales = 155
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM metric_translations WHERE description IS NULL — must be 0
No banned words in descriptions: diyabet, kanser, anemi, lösemi, siroz, hastalık, hastaligi, tehlikeli, ciddi
npm run build passes
PR created
